summary: Frontend-only verification of image URL normalizer fix for Decrypt/study card images. Tested local Next.js build at http://localhost:3100. All 4 study card images on the homepage /fr and on /fr/studies render successfully with complete=true and naturalWidth>0. All /api/files/ src attributes are RELATIVE (no https://*.emergentagent.com host on /api/files/ URLs). Same-origin proxy fetch through Vercel-style rewrite returns 200 image/png. Fix is in effect on localhost build.
action_items: Deploy/push to Vercel so iranobservatory.org gets the fix (user does this manually via 'Save to GitHub').
critical_code_review_comments: imageUrl.js: normalizer correctly rewrites *.emergentagent.com/api/* absolute URLs to relative /api/* paths and leaves other external URLs (customer-assets.emergentagent.com/job_*, pbs.twimg.com, etc.) untouched. Logic looks correct.

Article detail pages currently store external CDN URLs (pbs.twimg.com) as the hero image, so the relative /api/files/ rewrite is not exercised on those particular articles. That is expected — the normalizer still leaves them as-is and they load fine (naturalWidth=1200 verified). If business logic later requires those to be proxied/cached via Vercel, the normalizer would need an additional rule.
Console warns about preload of https://pbs.twimg.com/media/HLgcnNmWcAA0ALI.jpg not being used promptly — minor performance hint, not a bug.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all assertions on the bug fix passed)

seed_data_creation: None — used existing studies/articles from backend.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Local Next.js server started on port 3100 via `cd /app/frontend-next && yarn start -p 3100`. Public URL iranobservatory.org has NOT received this fix until user pushes to GitHub. Image normalizer: /app/frontend-next/lib/imageUrl.js. Verified /api/files/ proxy returns 200 image/png via direct fetch from localhost:3100. Homepage /fr loads 4 /api/files/ study images (naturalWidths 1003, 800, 1240, 984). /fr/studies loads same 4 images. Article detail heroes use external pbs.twimg.com URLs which are intentionally not rewritten and they render OK.

article_detail.note: Tested 3 articles. All hero images use pbs.twimg.com (external). Loaded OK (nw=1200). No /api/files/ images encountered on these article detail pages — expected, since those articles don't store backend-uploaded hero images.
